Key University Officials.Dean of FICS: Name not explicitly provided in source set (external verification needed).

General Admissions Policies: Eligibility includes Philippine-based applicants, offshore-based applicants, foreign nationals, and undergraduate transfer students.
Concurrent enrollment in two or more degree programs is not allowed.
Applicants must not be currently enrolled (or on LOA/AWOL) in another institution and must be cleared by previously attended institutions.
Hard copies of all admission documents are strictly required via mail.
UPOU cannot assist foreign applicants with Student Visa (9F) processing because residence in the Philippines is not required.

pairs.Restricted to 1st trimester/semester onl: AADDA, ASIT, AADE, BAMS, DCS, GCAS/MAS, MIS, MDC, DIH/MIH, MAN, DCOMM, D/MRDM, DLUP.

items: UPOU operates both semestral and trimesteral systems.
Trimester programs include all undergraduate programs, MDE, and GCDE.
Semester programs include most Graduate Certificate, Diploma, Master's, and Doctorate programs.

Important Deadlines.3rd Trimester AY 2025-2026: January 24, 2026 (Undergrad AA, ASIDT, BES) and February 21, 2026 (PTC, GCDE, MDE).
Important Deadlines.1st Semester AY 2026-2027: March 31, 2026 (tentative for programs with admission tests, other programs, and transfer students).
Important Deadlines.1st Trimester AY 2026-2027: March 31, 2026 (tentative per OAS) or May 30, 2026 (tentative per OUR).

pairs.UgAT AY 2025-2026 dates: July 5, 2025 (1st Trim), November 22, 2025 (2nd Trim), March 28, 2026 (3rd Trim).
pairs.UgAT AY 2026-2027 tentative dates: July 25, 2026 (1st Trim), November 14, 2026 (2nd Trim), February 27, 2027 (3rd Trim).
pairs.MPM-GAT: November 15, 2025 for 2nd Semester AY 2025-2026, fee PhP 500.00.

items: UgAT is required for high school graduates and ALS A&E passers applying to Associate programs; qualified transferees are exempt.
UPCAT is required for new incoming freshmen applying to Bachelor's programs; AA graduates, second-degree applicants, and qualified transferees are exempt.

Standard Documentary Requirements: Applicants must apply via OAS then mail hard copies to the Office of the University Registrar.
Signed hard copy of generated application form.
Original Official Transcript of Records with valid remarks.
One 2x2 colored ID photo with white/off-white background, taken within four months, with handwritten name tag and signature.
AADDA, ASIT, and AADE applicants must submit a 500-word essay on motivation and future plans.
Graduate/Post-Graduate applicants must submit two letters of recommendation.
Undergraduate applicants must complete DERM before admission.

pairs.From a UP unit: at least 30 units with GWA 2.5 or better.
pairs.From a non-UP unit: at least 33 units with GWA 2.0 or better.

items: PE, NSTP, theology, and technical courses are excluded from unit and GWA computations.

Financial Information.Tuition: PhP 1,000.00 per unit across all levels.
Financial Information.Miscellaneous fees: PhP 2,000.00 per term (Energy 250, Library 1,100, Registration 40, Technology 610).
Financial Information.New student fees: ID fee PhP 130, deposit PhP 100, entrance fee PhP 30.
Financial Information.Payment channels: UPOU online payment gateway (cards), Landbank Link.BizPortal (Landbank/Bancnet/GCash), or cash payment at UPOU Cash Office.

Underloading and Overloading Policies.Underloading: Undergraduate students below 12 units (excluding PE and CWTS) must apply in ORS.
Underloading and Overloading Policies.Overloading: Undergraduate students above 12 units or graduate students above 6 units must apply in ORS.

Key Contact Information.Mailing address: Admission Section, Office of the University Registrar, UP Open University, Los Baños, Laguna 4031, Philippines.
